actions: Initiate technical vetting by Priya (tech lead) for both Indian contract engineers before any contract is signed — this internal review is a mandatory prerequisite to contract execution per Helix policy

Use IC agreement template v2.1 (international version) for both engineers — this template includes the required W-8BEN-E provisions and mandatory 30-day notice clause; do not substitute MSA v3.2 or any domestic IC template

Include explicit no-equity language in both contracts: per Helix's post-2023 cap table cleanup policy, contractors engaged for exactly 6 months are at the boundary — confirm with Marcus whether these 6-month engagements qualify for equity eligibility; if not confirmed as over 6 months, default to excluding equity and state this clearly in the agreement

Collect W-8BEN-E forms from both engineers prior to first payment for tax compliance

Confirm engagement start and end dates precisely — if either engagement is fewer than 6 months, equity exclusion is unambiguous; if exactly or over 6 months, flag for Marcus to make an explicit equity decision
File both contractor records for potential future compliance review given India-based cross-border classification

Speed pressure ('set them up quickly') creates risk of skipping sequential mandatory steps (Priya vetting → contract signing → Marcus payment approval) — all three are non-negotiable and must remain in sequence
If either engineer is later found to be misclassified or has undisclosed IP encumbrances from other biotech roles, Helix could face IP ownership disputes or contractor misclassification liability

Paying through Mercury instead of Wise for international transfers would result in excessive fees and violates current Helix policy — ensure finance team is briefed on the Wise-only rule

Misclassification risk: if either engineer works in a manner resembling an employee (set hours, exclusive engagement, direct supervision), the 2023 misclassification precedent requires this be reviewed before finalizing contractor status
If W-8BEN-E forms are not collected before payment, Helix may face US withholding tax obligations on payments to foreign contractors — do not process first payment without forms on file
